title: BossFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing boss features in a design.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a new boss feature (or more boss features) based on the information provided by a BossFeatureInput object. To create a new boss or boss connection, use createInput function to define a new input object for the type of boss feature you want to create. Use the methods and properties on the input object to define any additional inputs. Once the information is defined on the input object, you can pass it to the Add method to create the boss feature or boss connection.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a new BossFeatureInput object that is used to specify the input needed to create a new boss feature(s).

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified boss feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified boss feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of boss features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Boss Feature Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new boss feature

add.title: BossFeatures.add Method
add.description: Creates a new boss feature (or more boss features) based on the information provided by a BossFeatureInput object. To create a new boss or boss connection, use createInput function to define a new input object for the type of boss feature you want to create. Use the methods and properties on the input object to define any additional inputs. Once the information is defined on the input object, you can pass it to the Add method to create the boss feature or boss connection.
add.return_description: Returns the newly created BossFeature objects or empty vector/list if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The BossFeatureInput object that defines the boss or boss connection you want to create.

related_samples.name: Boss Feature Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new boss feature

classType.title: BossFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: BossFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a new BossFeatureInput object that is used to specify the input needed to create a new boss feature(s).
createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created BossFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

related_samples.name: Boss Feature Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new boss feature

item.title: BossFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified boss feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: BossFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified boss feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: BossFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of boss features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = bossFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: BossFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = bossFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: BossFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = bossFeatures_var.objectType
